items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Macduff's Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* fife perthshire
*County:* Fife
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 14th-16th century
*Coords:* 56.16222222, -3.05805556
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Macduff%27s_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Macduff's%20Castle%20Fife%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/1/1a/Macduff%27s_Castle_and_the_Wemyss_Caves.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
MacDuff's Castle is a ruined castle near East Wemyss, in Fife, Scotland. The site is associated with the MacDuff Earls of Fife, the most powerful family in Fife in the Middle Ages, although nothing survives from this period. The present ruins are the remains of the home of the Wemyss family, who lived here from the 14th century, and their successors in the 16th century.

_Listing 2 of 10_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Balgonie Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* fife perthshire
*County:* Fife
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 14th century
*Coords:* 56.1938, -3.1092
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Balgonie_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Balgonie%20Castle%20Fife%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/51/BalgonieCastle04.jpg/330px-BalgonieCastle04.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Balgonie Castle is located on the south bank of the River Leven near Milton of Balgonie, 3.5 kilometres (2.2 mi) east of Glenrothes, Fife, Scotland. The castle keep dates from the 14th century, and the remaining structures were added piecemeal until the 18th century. The keep has been recently restored, although other parts of the castle are roofless ruins. Balgonie, excepting the tower which is used for residential purposes, is a Scheduled Ancient Monument. This castle is the subject for several ghostlore stories, including a green lady story.

_Listing 5 of 10_

listing.name: Balgonie Castle
listing.desc: Balgonie Castle is located on the south bank of the River Leven near Milton of Balgonie, 3.5 kilometres (2.2 mi) east of Glenrothes, Fife, Scotland. The keep has been recently restored, although other parts of the castle are roofless ruins. Balgonie, excepting the tower which is used for residential purposes, is a Scheduled Ancient Monument. This castle is the subject for several ghostlore stories, including a green lady story.

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Cathcart Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* glasgow stirling
*County:* Glasgow
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 15th century
*Coords:* 55.813, -4.2563
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cathcart_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Cathcart%20Castle%20Glasgow%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/cathcart-castle.jpg
*Gallery:* 4 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* google places link - https://maps.app.goo.gl/zyBLRKZxQdinVKFr5

*Description:*
Cathcart Castle was a 15th-century castle, located in what is now Linn Park in the Cathcart area of southern Glasgow, Scotland. The castle was abandoned in the 18th century, and the remaining ruins were pulled down in 1980, leaving only foundations visible.

_Listing 6 of 10_
